req.title: System-Admin views benefit
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a benefit through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-036 requires a system-admin to view a benefit via GET /api/benefits. Three core risk areas drive test selection: (1) happy-path retrieval with audit verification (EP – valid partition), (2) unauthenticated access rejection (NEG), (3) boundary on benefit ID at the minimum valid value (BVA). Additional cases cover role-based access control (DT) and module-disabled state transition (STC) to reach the required minimum of five tests and give broad coverage of the stated error pathways.

tests.name: test_system_admin_can_view_benefit_and_audit_is_written
preconditions: At least one benefit record exists with a known id
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <get_admin_token fixture>
Retrieve a known benefit id via <seed_benefit fixture>
Capture response and audit log entry via <get_latest_audit fixture>
expected: Response body contains the correct entity id
An audit record is written referencing the benefit id and actor
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_system_admin_can_view_benefit_and_audit_is_written(
    admin_token, seed_benefit, api_client, get_latest_audit
):
    benefit_id = seed_benefit["id"]
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/api/benefits/{benefit_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["id"] == benefit_id

    audit = get_latest_audit(resource="benefit", resource_id=benefit_id)
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit["action"] == "VIEW"
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "admin"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
preconditions: At least one benefit record exists
actions: Retrieve a known benefit id via <seed_benefit fixture>
Send GET /api/benefits/{benefit_id} without any Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an error message indicating unauthenticated access
No audit record is written for this request
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(
    seed_benefit, api_client, get_latest_audit
):
    benefit_id = seed_benefit["id"]
    resp = api_client.get(f"/api/benefits/{benefit_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body

    audit = get_latest_audit(resource="benefit", resource_id=benefit_id)
    assert audit is None or audit.get("action") != "VIEW"

tests.name: test_view_benefit_with_minimum_valid_id_boundary
preconditions: A benefit with the minimum allowable numeric id (1) exists
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <get_admin_token fixture>
Ensure a benefit with id=1 exists via <seed_benefit_with_id(1) fixture>
expected: An audit record is written for benefit id 1
trace: BVA: lower boundary of benefit id domain
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_view_benefit_with_minimum_valid_id_boundary(
    admin_token, seed_benefit_with_id, api_client, get_latest_audit
):
    seed_benefit_with_id(benefit_id=1)
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/benefits/1",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["id"] == 1

    audit = get_latest_audit(resource="benefit", resource_id=1)
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit["action"] == "VIEW"

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_forbidden_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but holds the role 'employee' (not admin)
At least one benefit record exists
actions: Obtain an auth token for a non-admin user via <get_employee_token fixture>
Retrieve a known benefit id via <seed_benefit fixture>
Send GET /api/benefits/{benefit_id} with the employee token
expected: Response body contains a forbidden or permission-denied message
No audit record is written for this request
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_admin_role_forbidden_returns_403(
    employee_token, seed_benefit, api_client, get_latest_audit
):
    benefit_id = seed_benefit["id"]
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/api/benefits/{benefit_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body

    audit = get_latest_audit(resource="benefit", resource_id=benefit_id)
    assert audit is None or audit.get("action") != "VIEW"

tests.name: test_view_benefit_when_module_disabled_returns_error
preconditions: Benefit module is DISABLED via <disable_benefit_module fixture>
A benefit record exists from a prior enabled state
actions: Obtain a valid admin auth token via <get_admin_token fixture>
Disable the benefit module via <disable_benefit_module fixture>
Re-enable the benefit module via <enable_benefit_module fixture> for cleanup
expected: HTTP status code is 400 or 403 or 404 (module disabled error)
Response body indicates the benefit module is not available
trace: R-WEB-036: precondition benefit module is enabled – transition to disabled state
STC: enabled->disabled state transition blocks access
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_view_benefit_when_module_disabled_returns_error(
    admin_token, seed_benefit, api_client,
    disable_benefit_module, enable_benefit_module, get_latest_audit
):
    benefit_id = seed_benefit["id"]
    disable_benefit_module()
    try:
        resp = api_client.get(
            f"/api/benefits/{benefit_id}",
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"},
        )
        assert resp.status_code in (400, 403, 404)
        body = resp.json()
        assert "error" in body or "detail" in body

        audit = get_latest_audit(resource="benefit", resource_id=benefit_id)
        assert audit is None or audit.get("action") != "VIEW"
    finally:
        enable_benefit_module()
